title: fix(sdk): use model catalog category field
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
- Adds `category` to model endpoint metadata from the registry.
- Updates SDK model metadata typing to include category.
- Makes `fetchModelCatalog` use endpoint `category` as the source of truth, with legacy modality fallback only when category is absent.
- Adds focused SDK tests for category handling.

Checks:
- `npx biome check --write shared/registry/model-info.ts packages/sdk/src/types.ts packages/sdk/src/models.ts packages/sdk/src/models.test.ts`
- `npm run test --prefix packages/sdk -- models.test.ts`
- `npm run typecheck --prefix packages/sdk`
- `npm run build --prefix packages/sdk`
- `npm run test --prefix packages/sdk`

gist.headline: Categorically speaking
gist.blurb: We've updated the SDK to use a proper category field for models instead of the legacy modality system. Cleaner types, better model sorting, and a fresh coat of paint for the UI components.
gist.summary: The SDK's fetchModelCatalog now uses the registry's category field as the source of truth for model metadata, replacing the legacy modality system, alongside a major refactor of the UI package components.
gist.impact: Developers using the SDK will see a new category field in model metadata, and the React UI components have been streamlined with new Typography, CodeBlock, and StatCard primitives.
gist.visual_concept: The bee mascot acting as a librarian or organizer, sorting glowing orbs (models) into neatly labeled categories. The robot helper assisting with the new labeling system.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing tiny librarian glasses, organizing glowing pixelated orbs into neatly labeled wooden cubbies. A chunky robot with a CRT monitor head holds a lime green (#ecf874) label reading 'CATEGORY'. Soft pastel gradient background, warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley.
